steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says similarities in isotopic signatures make it tempting to treat meteorite geochemistry as ______ Mars's interior — but doing so is inadvisable. The blank describes a role meteorites would play: standing in as substitutes for direct samples of Mars's interior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — proxies for: A proxy is a substitute that stands in for something inaccessible. Treating meteorites as proxies for Mars's interior is exactly the tempting but flawed move the paper cautions against. ✓
  • Choice B — contrivances of: A contrivance is an artificial device or scheme. Meteorites aren't manufactured products of Mars's interior — this makes no scientific sense here. ✗
  • Choice C — catalysts of: A catalyst triggers a reaction or change. Nothing in the passage suggests meteorites cause or trigger Mars's interior — they're used as evidence, not agents. ✗
  • Choice D — deterrents to: A deterrent discourages or prevents. This reverses the logic — the passage says scientists are tempted toward using meteorites, not deterred by them. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
